title: paspalum ammodes
intro: Paspalum ammodes, also known as Paspalum, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Paspalum ammodes include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a diuretic. Paspalum ammodes can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, or as a tincture by soaking the plant in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
